title: Hattie the Mouse and the Starry Hats
summary: Hattie the mouse is interrupted in her workshop late at night by a group of cold forest creatures—baby raccoons, foxes, and owls—who are in desperate need of hats. While Hattie has mushrooms, glue, and paper, she is missing the essential twinkling star stickers to finish the job. After a journey to Mama Fox’s burrow to secure the stickers, Hattie crafts beautiful hats for every animal. Once the work is done, Hattie overcomes a case of the 'can't-fall-asleep blues' by following her nightly bedtime routine and finally settles in for a peaceful rest.
audioFilename: Stars and Stories - Hats for Baby Creatures.m4a

highlightQuote.text: Mushrooms! Hattie already had mushrooms, and Hattie already had glue, and Hattie already had paper, and Hattie already had tape, but she doesn't have little twinkling star stickers.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Can we just start about Hattie?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Is this story always going to be about Hattie?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, it is.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Another story about Hattie in her workshop making buttons.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Let's begin. Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse was sitting in her workshop and instead of one little knock on the door, there were lots and lots and lots of little knocks.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nobody was answering the door.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But nobody was answering the door and so Hattie got up and said, hmm, I wonder who's knocking like that at my door.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nobody. Everyone was at their house sleeping. Because it's their bedtime.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it was nighttime?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, so everyone was asleep. So nobody was knocking at Hattie's door.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie said to herself, gosh, it's bedtime, and everybody's in their house sleeping, so I wonder who could be knocking at my door and I don't see anyone. But I heard lots and lots of little knocks like this. Maybe someone very small is knocking at my door. Who could it be?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But everyone was still sleeping.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, some creatures, Hattie said to herself, do stay awake at night.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Like owls and raccoons.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And she looked all around, up and to the left and to the right, and she didn't see anyone, and then you know where she looked?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Hattie, they're just owls and raccoons outside. They stay awake.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They do stay awake at night. And she looked down at her feet and she saw some tiny furry creatures. That's who it was that was knocking. It was a whole family of baby raccoons. And how...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And foxes and little owls.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And foxes and little owls, baby owls too. How many of them were there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ten.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There were ten baby raccoons and some foxes and owls. And they said, oh, finally you noticed! We're very small. We heard that you know how to make hats. Is that true? And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It is.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, please, we don't have any hats and it's getting cold out, so we would like to have some hats to keep our heads warm. Can we come in?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Hattie said, I don't have anything to make hats, I have nothing to make small hats for you too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You don't? Oh no!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You might have to, you might have to ask your mom about that question. Oh, and your mom is Mom Fox, and she's in her burrow sleeping.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, she is?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But she's awake now. So they had to run as fast as they can, all those foxes to get to Mom.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And across that last street over there and then you have to turn right. That's the way to get to your mom.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Well, will you come with us?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's what the baby mouse and fox said. And the squirrels too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so the whole group ran across the street and took a right and then they went up the hill a little ways to a little hole in the ground that led to the underground burrow of Mama Fox.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah and they said, hello Mama, can you please make me a small hat?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mama Fox said, oh, I was just about to go to sleep little ones. You'd like a hat would you? Well, I expect you've already visited Hattie the mouse's house. She's the one that makes hats. Why doesn't she make one for you?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because she doesn't have the things to make hats.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She doesn't have the things to make a hat. What does she need do you think?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mushrooms! Hattie already had mushrooms, and Hattie already had glue, and Hattie already had paper, and Hattie already had tape, but she doesn't have little twinkling star stickers.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, twinkling star stickers! said Mama Fox.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And let me buy some. And so I buy some. She bought them, all of them. And then she gave all of them to Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then Hattie put mushrooms, glue, and she made all of them. And then she gave one big one for Mom too.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then all the animals, even the squirrels, had their own hats too. And then Hattie put on her hat.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wow! And then Hattie had twenty more mushrooms.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank goodness for that.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie has twenty more mushrooms.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie has plenty more mushrooms. And now all the baby raccoons and the baby foxes and the baby owls and even the squirrels have their own...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And the Mama Fox.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mama Fox has a hat too. And every one of those little hats has a sparkly star sticker. And Hattie the mouse said, wow, now it's really getting late. It must be almost time for bed. Did I do everything that you need baby raccoon? And what did baby raccoon say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. Oh, let's start the stars and stories time for yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We have time for one more very short Stars and Stories story about Hattie. And this is the Hattie going to sleep story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, actually it's the can't-fall-asleep blues Hattie story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah. So Hattie was very tired and so what did she do first to get ready for bed?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: In that falling to sleep one or the can't-fall-asleep blues Hattie one?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what happens first in that story?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She said, hm, I don't know how to get my blues to go.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Hattie the mouse says, gosh, I better do all the things that I do every night to get ready for bed because those are the things that help me get sleepy.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: First she read a book and then she got undressed and then she brushed her teeth and then she peed in the potty.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She did those stories and then she read a book and she said your mama came and said goodnight and then she went in her room and she went on her bed and she got her blankets and her fluffiness and her shoes, sparkly pink socks and slippers and all the things she needed to get and then she got on her blankets and then she got her stuffies and then she was all ready to go to bed.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then she was all ready to go to bed. And so her mama said goodnight Hattie the mouse, and her papa said goodnight Hattie the mouse, and then they turned off the light and shut the door and did Hattie the mouse go to sleep?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie the mouse went to sleep. Okay, that's the end.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want to take out the lantern.

characters.name: Hattie
characters.description: A clever mouse milliner and engineer of whimsy, she is a resourceful spirit who crafts exquisite hats and soulful portraits within her hollow tree stump workshop. Though a formidable presence famously counted among the thirty legendary cats, she is currently confined to her bed by illness, her usual treks through saturated woodlands for rare tape and lollipops temporarily halted. Even in her malaise, her sharp mind lingers on a recent, stinging disagreement with Dimitar over a fish, a rare shadow cast upon a heart otherwise known for its boundless generosity and creative light.

characters.name: Baby Raccoons
characters.description: A family of ten small, nocturnal creatures who seek hats from Hattie.

characters.name: Mama Fox
characters.description: A fox who lives in an underground burrow and provides star stickers for the hats.

characters.name: Baby Foxes
characters.description: Small foxes who join the group seeking hats to keep warm.

characters.name: Baby Owls
characters.description: Tiny nocturnal birds who also receive starry hats from Hattie.

characters.name: Squirrels
characters.description: Forest animals who join the others to get their own hats.

characters.name: Hattie's Mama
characters.description: Hattie the mouse's mother who says goodnight to her.

characters.name: Hattie's Papa
characters.description: Hattie the mouse's father who says goodnight at bedtime.

places.name: Hattie's Workshop
places.description: Tucked within the cozy hollow of a weathered tree, Hattie Mouse’s dwelling serves as a busy, imaginative atelier and social hub where the air hums with the rhythmic snip of scissors. Here, amidst a cheerful clutter of measuring tapes, wet pigments, and scavenged shells, she crafts her signature starry hats from an eclectic mix of fur and feathers. The workshop is bathed in the earthy radiance of her fungal store—a brilliant collection of red, orange, and yellow mushrooms—illuminating the haunting indigo witch painting where Hattie first encountered the squeaking worm. It is in this storied corner, beneath the witch's gaze, that she recently unearthed a mysterious message hidden alongside a cache of sparkly green clover stickers.

places.name: Mama Fox's Burrow
places.description: An underground home up a hill where Mama Fox sleeps and stores star stickers.

places.name: Hattie's Bedroom
places.description: Where Hattie performs her bedtime routine and eventually falls asleep.
